---
doc-id: aion-http-module-v1
title: Tehnična specifikacija modula `http`
purpose: API definicije, tipi in pravila za varno izvajanje omrežnih zahtev v Aionu.
tags: stdlib, http, network, async, match, mock
last-updated: 2026-03-17
---

# Modul `http` v standardni knjižnici Aion

**Bottom Line Up Front (BLUF):** Modul `http` v jeziku Aion omogoča asinhrono izvajanje omrežnih zahtev s pomočjo deklarativnih cevovodov (`|`). Zaradi nepredvidljive narave omrežnih klicev modul na ravni prevajalnika zahteva strogo strukturirano ujemanje stanj (`match`), s čimer umetno inteligenco (LLM) prisili k eksplicitnemu obravnavanju vseh možnih izidov (npr. HTTP statusov 404 in 500), kar preprečuje uhajanje neobravnavanih izjem [1].

## 1. Podatkovne strukture (Structs)

Modul `http` deluje z vnaprej določenimi, strojno optimiziranimi strukturami. Umetna inteligenca pri klicih znotraj prototipiranja običajno uporablja tip `infer`, ki ga zaledje MLIR samodejno razreši [2].

### 1.1 `Response`
Struktura, ki jo vrne vsak HTTP klic.
- `status: Int32`: HTTP statusna koda (npr. 200, 404, 500).
- `body: String`: Surovo besedilo odgovora.
- `headers: Map[String, String]`: Slovar prejetih HTTP glav.
- **Metode:**
  - `json(self) -> infer?`: Poskusi razčleniti `body` kot JSON. Vrne opcijski (nullable) tip `infer?`, ki prevzame tip glede na kontekst leve strani enačbe. Če razčlenjevanje ne uspe, vrne `null`.
  - `text(self) -> String`: Vrne surovo besedilo (enako kot `body`).

### 1.2 `RequestOptions`
Struktura za podajanje dodatnih parametrov zahtevku.
- `headers: Map[String, String]?`: Opcijske glave zahtevka (npr. `{"Authorization": "Bearer token"}`).
- `body: infer?`: Telo zahtevka (lahko je surovi `String` ali JSON struktura).
- `timeout: Int32?`: Časovna omejitev v milisekundah (privzeto 5000).

## 2. Metode modula

Vse metode se izvajajo asinhrono. Ker Aion abstrahira zapleteno večnitnost preko cevovodov (pipeline orchestration) [3], LLM agentu ni treba generirati in upravljati ključnih besed, kot so `async/await` ali `Thread::spawn`.

- `http.get(url: String, options: RequestOptions? = null) -> Response`
- `http.post(url: String, options: RequestOptions? = null) -> Response`
- `http.put(url: String, options: RequestOptions? = null) -> Response`
- `http.delete(url: String, options: RequestOptions? = null) -> Response`

*(Agent Note: Pri podajanju opcij v klicu lahko ustvarite anonimno strukturo: `http.post(url, {body: my_data})`)*.

## 3. Varnost in obravnava napak (Exhaustive Pattern Matching)

AI modeli v tradicionalnih jezikih (npr. Python) pogosto napačno predpostavijo, da bo omrežni klic vedno vrnil veljaven odgovor, kar vodi do pogostih `AttributeError` in `TypeError` zrušitev [4]. 

Aion ta problem rešuje arhitekturno. Kadar koda izvede `http.get`, mora rezultat **obvezno** preiti skozi `match` blok. Prevajalnik pri tem zahteva popolno pokritost vseh mogočih stanj. Če LLM ne definira obravnave za primere napak ali ne uporabi rezervnega ("catch-all") pogoja `_ =>`, koda ne bo prevedena [1].

## 4. Nativna validacija z `mock` ukazi

Ker Aion vključuje t. i. "vericoding" proces [5], kjer se izvajalna logika preverja že v času kompilacije, zunanji API klici znotraj izoliranega preizkusnega okolja (sandboxa) nimajo dostopa do zunanjega interneta. Zato modul `http` integrira domorodno podporo za nadomeščanje klicev (mocking) neposredno v `verify` bloku [1, 6].

## 5. Primer: Implementacija API klica s specifikacijo `http.post`

Spodnji primer prikazuje, kako AI agent s pomočjo Aionove sintakse zgradi, opiše in matematično preveri proces ustvarjanja novega uporabnika na oddaljenem strežniku.

```aion
#intent
# goal: "Pošlji uporabniške podatke na API. Vrni generiran ID. Če pride do napake pri validaciji (400), vrni null."
# pre: "payload == UserStruct"
# post: "return == String? AND handles 201, 400 and general server errors explicitly"
fn create_remote_user(payload:UserStruct)->String?{
    
    // Klic se izvede in pretaka (pipe) v obravnavo izidov [3]
    http.post("/api/v1/users", {body: payload})|match{
        201 => ?.json().user_id as String,
        400 => null, 
        _   => throw APIError("Unexpected network or server error")
    }

    // Nativna validacija - enotni testi znotraj definicije funkcije [5]
    verify{
        // Priprava izoliranega okolja (mock) za simulacijo omrežja [1]
        mock http.post("/api/v1/users", *) => Response(201, '{"user_id": "usr_99X"}')
        mock http.post("/api/v1/users", {body: null}) => Response(400, '{"error": "bad request"}')
        mock http.post("/api/v1/users", *) => Response(500, '')

        let valid_user = UserStruct(name: "Ada")
        
        // Testiranje uspešnega klica
        assert create_remote_user(valid_user) == "usr_99X"
        
        // Testiranje varnega ravnanja z Null tipi ob napaki [1, 7]
        assert create_remote_user(null) == null
        
        // Preverjanje padca ob nedefiniranem izidu
        assert_throws(APIError){ create_remote_user(valid_user) }
    }
}
Kako koda izpolnjuje Aionove standarde:
Brez vizualnega balasta (Minimally Tokenized Syntax): Za prehajanje med funkcijami se uporabljajo deterministični, gosti operatorji (|, ?., =>), presledki in nove vrstice niso del gramatike prevajalnika
.
Odkrivanje napak vnaprej: Povratni tip funkcije je strogo preverjen kot opcijski String?. Ker preverjanje poteka v izoliranem modulu v času kompilacije, LLM ne more generirati rešitve, ki bi sistemu povzročila nepredvidene poškodbe ob prekinitvi povezave.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
---
doc-id: aion-stdlib-v1
title: Aion Standard Library Reference
purpose: API definitions, type signatures, and usage examples for Aion core modules.
tags: stdlib, core, http, array, string, pipeline
last-updated: 2026-03-17
---

# Aion Standard Library (StdLib)

**Bottom Line Up Front (BLUF):** This document defines the core types (`Array`, `String`), declarative pipeline operators (`map`, `filter`), and the `http` module for the Aion programming language. All examples utilize Aion's Minimally Tokenized Syntax, mandatory `#intent` blocks, and `verify` validation loops.

## 1. Core Data Types

### 1.1 `Array[T]`
Aion arrays are strongly typed, dynamically sized contiguous memory buffers. Under the hood, they are lowered to MLIR `linalg` dialects for hardware-agnostic vectorized operations.

**Signatures:**
- `len(self) -> Int32`: Returns the number of elements in the array.
- `push(self, item: T) -> Void`: Appends an item to the end.
- `pop(self) -> T?`: Removes and returns the last item. Returns `null` if empty.

**AI Agent Usage Note:** Always handle the `null` case when calling `pop()` due to Aion's strict null safety.

### 1.2 `String`
UTF-8 encoded text sequences. Strings in Aion are immutable.

**Signatures:**
- `contains(self, substring: String) -> Bool`: Checks if the substring exists.
- `split(self, delimiter: String) -> Array[String]`: Splits the string by the given delimiter.
- `replace(self, old: String, new: String) -> String`: Returns a new string with replacements.

## 2. Declarative Pipeline Operators

Aion relies on declarative pipelines (`|`) instead of imperative loops to process data collections. These operators automatically support asynchronous execution and are evaluated lazily.

### 2.1 `map(transform: Fn(T) -> U)`
Transforms each element in an `Array[T]` into type `U`.

### 2.2 `filter(predicate: Fn(T) -> Bool)`
Returns a new `Array[T]` containing only elements where the predicate returns `true`.

**Example: Processing an Array**
```aion
#intent
# goal: "Filter even numbers and multiply them by 10."
# pre: "numbers == Array[Int32]"
# post: "return == Array[Int32] where all elements are multiples of 20"
fn process_numbers(numbers:Array[Int32])->Array[Int32]{
    numbers|filter(?.%2==0)|map(?.*10)
}
(Agent Note: ?. is the implicit pipeline element accessor).
3. Network Module: http
The http module provides safe, asynchronous network requests. Because network calls are inherently unpredictable, Aion enforces strict pattern matching on HTTP responses.
3.1 http.get(url: String) -> Response
Performs an asynchronous HTTP GET request.
Struct Response:
status: Int32: The HTTP status code.
body: String: The raw response body.
json(self) -> infer?: Parses the body as JSON. Returns an unresolved type (infer) that the compiler autotunes based on the left-hand assignment. Returns null if parsing fails.
Example: Safe API Consumption
#intent
# goal: "Fetch data from an API. Return the parsed struct or null if not found (404)."
# pre: "url == String"
# post: "return == DataStruct? AND all HTTP errors are handled"
fn fetch_secure_data(url:String)->DataStruct?{
    
    let res = http.get(url)
    
    res|match{
        200=>?.json() as DataStruct,
        404=>null,
        _=>throw NetworkError("API Failure")
    }

    verify{
        mock http.get("test.com/ok") => Response(200, '{"id": 1}')
        mock http.get("test.com/fail") => Response(404, '')
        assert fetch_secure_data("test.com/ok") != null
        assert fetch_secure_data("test.com/fail") == null
    }
}

### Analiza strukture na podlagi virov:
1. **Oznake in klasifikacija (YAML Frontmatter):** Blok na začetku dokumenta zagotavlja eksplicitne metapodatke (`doc-id`, `tags`, `purpose`), kar je po standardih MAGI (Markdown for Agent Guidance & Instruction) ključno za agentne sisteme in izboljšano RAG iskanje [5, 6].
2. **Kompaktnost (Markdown):** Celoten API je zapisan v Markdownu, ki je za modele najbolj optimalen, saj je sintaktično lahek in preprečuje izgubo žetonov v primerjavi s formatom HTML ali PDF [11-13].
3. **Pomen primerov:** Vključeni so strukturirani primeri kode s FQL preverjanjem (`#intent`) in enotnimi testi (`verify`). Raziskave na področju RAG sistemov priporočajo, da dokumentacija API-jev vsebuje predvsem raznolike primere uporabe, saj to preprečuje, da bi LLM napačno sklepal o delovanju knjižnic [1, 2, 8].
4. **Agent Notes (Opombe za agente):** Besedilo vključuje opozorila, ustvarjena neposredno za model (npr. opozorilo o nujnosti obravnavanja `null` pri odstranjevanju iz polja), s čimer model ob branju dokumentacije takoj asimilira varnostna pravila jezika Aion in preprečuje napake `NullReferenceException`, ki so pogoste v dinamičnih jezikih [14-16].

# Aion Programming Language - Full Documentation

> Aion is the world's first AI-native programming language designed specifically for Large Language Models and agentic workflows. It features a Minimally Tokenized Syntax, Gradual Verification via a Formal Query Language (FQL), AST-First Contextualization, and hardware-agnostic autotuning via an MLIR backend.
> This file contains the complete, flattened technical documentation for the Aion language, designed for full context window ingestion by LLMs.

---

## 1. Philosophy and Architecture

**The Paradigm Shift:** Aion transitions software engineering from a "Human-Readable Era" to an "AI-Generative Era." Legacy languages (Python, Rust) prioritize visual hierarchies and mnemonic readability, which consume valuable context window budgets and compute on structurally meaningless characters. Aion replaces human-centric visual syntax with a Minimally Tokenized Syntax, reducing token overhead by up to 24.5%.

### Core Architectural Pillars:
- **Frontend (AST & Repository Map):** Parses AI-oriented grammar directly into Abstract Syntax Trees (AST). The compiler natively maintains a structural map of the entire codebase, giving the LLM global context of dependencies.
- **Intent-Based Semantics (FQL):** Aion enforces the use of a Formal Query Language (FQL) via mandatory `#intent` blocks. The compiler uses gradual verification to mathematically prove that the generated AST matches the FQL intent.
- **Backend (MLIR):** The AST is lowered into a Multi-Level Intermediate Representation (MLIR). This provides hardware-agnostic autotuning, offloading all low-level micro-optimizations (e.g., CUDA memory management) to the compiler.
- **Declarative Pipeline Orchestration:** Aion uses a "pipe-first" architecture using the `|` operator. Data flows strictly from left to right, automatically supporting asynchronous and parallel execution.

---

## 2. Memory Management and Typing

Aion ensures memory safety without the cognitive load of Rust's borrow checker. It introduces **AI-Guided Ownership** utilizing gradual verification.

- **Gradual Verification:** Basic ownership rules apply. When the compiler cannot mathematically prove a complex lifetime is valid (a "Not Sure" state), it delegates a prompt back to the LLM, asking for an explicit semantic proof in the `#intent` block. If the LLM cannot satisfy the theorem prover, the compiler dynamically wraps the specific memory boundary in a deterministic garbage-collected sandbox.
- **Predictive Strong Typing:** 
  - *Drafting Phase:* The LLM can use the `infer` keyword for rapid prototyping.
  - *Compilation Phase:* The MLIR backend automatically resolves all `infer` placeholders into strict, hardware-optimized structs (e.g., `Int32`, `Float64`) during whole-program analysis.
- **Strict Null Safety:** Variables cannot be `null` unless explicitly marked nullable (e.g., `String?`). Unhandled nulls are rejected at compile time.

---

## 3. Core Syntax and Intentional Programming

Aion discards formatting spaces and newlines. Variable declarations use `let` (mutable) and `const` (immutable).

### The `#intent` Block
Every function must begin with a mandatory `#intent` block. The AI must describe its logic, expected inputs, and desired outcomes. If the executable code diverges from the constraints defined in the `#intent` block, the Aion compiler flags a semantic mismatch.

### Example: Data Filter Pipeline
```aion
#intent
# goal: "Filter a list of product records to find active items under a maximum price."
# pre: "data == Array[ProductStruct], max_price == Float64"
# post: "return == Array[ProductStruct] where ?.is_active == true AND ?.price <= max_price"
fn filter_active_products(data:Array[ProductStruct],max_price:Float64)->Array[ProductStruct]{
    data|filter(?.is_active==true)|filter(?.price<=max_price)
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
4. Validation and Error Handling
Aion integrates unit testing directly into the function definition using the verify keyword.
Native Validation Loops
The AI agent writes the #intent block.
The AI writes the declarative execution logic.
The AI writes the verify block containing concrete unit tests.
During compilation, Aion executes the verify block within an isolated, ephemeral sandbox.
If it fails, the compiler captures the runtime control-flow trace and feeds it back to the LLM for autonomous self-correction.
Example: Explicit API Error Handling
#intent
# goal: "Fetch user profile data. If 404, return null safely."
# pre: "user_id == String"
# post: "return == UserProfile?"
fn fetch_user_profile(user_id:String)->UserProfile?{
    http.get("/api/users/${user_id}")|match{
        200=>?.json() as UserProfile,
        404=>null, 
        _=>throw APIError("Unexpected status")
    }
    verify{
        mock http.get("/api/users/valid") => Response(200, "{...}")
        mock http.get("/api/users/missing") => Response(404)
        assert fetch_user_profile("valid") != null
        assert fetch_user_profile("missing") == null
    }
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
5. Formal Syntax Specification (EBNF ISO/IEC 14977)
Aion's grammar explicitly omits Whitespace, Indent, and Newline tokens to minimize tokenization fertility.
program = { declaration } ;
declaration = constant_decl | variable_decl | function_decl ;
type_name = "Int32" | "Float64" | "String" | "Bool" | "UserProfile" | "infer" | identifier ;
array_type = "Array", "[", type_name, "]" ;
base_type = type_name | array_type ;
type = base_type, [ "?" ] ; 
constant_decl = "const", identifier, ":", type, "=", expression ;
variable_decl = "let", identifier, ":", type, "=", expression ;
intent_block = "#intent", { intent_directive } ;
intent_directive = "#", ( "goal:" | "pre:" | "post:" | "proof:" ), string_literal ;
function_decl = intent_block, "fn", identifier, "(", [ parameter_list ], ")", "->", type, "{", execution_body, [ verify_block ], "}" ;
parameter_list = parameter, { ",", parameter } ;
parameter = identifier, ":", type ;
execution_body = { variable_decl }, pipeline_expr ;
pipeline_expr = expression, { "|", pipe_operation } ;
pipe_operation = function_call | match_block | method_chain ;
match_block = "match", "{", { match_case }, "}" ;
match_case = ( expression | "_" ), "=>", expression, "," ;
verify_block = "verify", "{", { verify_stmt }, "}" ;
verify_stmt = mock_stmt | assert_stmt ;
mock_stmt = "mock", function_call, "=>", expression ;
assert_stmt = "assert", expression | "assert_throws", "(", identifier, ")", "{", function_call, "}" ;
expression = identifier | literal | function_call | method_chain | binary_expr ;
function_call = identifier, "(", [ argument_list ], ")" ;
method_chain = "?.", identifier, [ "(", [ argument_list ], ")" ] ;
argument_list = expression, { ",", expression } ;
binary_expr = expression, operator, expression ;
identifier = letter, { letter | digit | "_" } ;
literal = string_literal | numeric_literal | "null" | "true" | "false" ;
operator = "==" | "!=" | "<" | "<=" | ">" | ">=" | "+" | "-" | "*" | "/" ;

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
6. Formal Semantics
Axiomatic Semantics for Intent Verification
Used to check compliance between FQL specifications and AST logic using Hoare triples {P} S {Q}: ∀x. P(x) => P^(x), ∀x,y. P(x) ∧ Q^(x,y) => Q(x,y) / valid_intent(f)
Operational Semantics (SOS) for LLVM IR Lowering
Predictive Strong Typing: Γ ⊢ e : T, T ≠ infer / Γ ⊢ let x : infer = e => LLVM_IR: %x = alloca T; store T e, ptr %x
Pipeline Orchestration: Γ ⊢ e ⇓ %v_1, Γ ⊢ f(%v_1) ⇓ %v_2 / Γ ⊢ e | f => %v_2
Null Safety Branching: Γ ⊢ e : T?, T? is nullable / Γ ⊢ match e { null => e_1, x => e_2 } => LLVM_IR: br_on_null e, label_null, label_val

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
7. MLIR Aion High-Level Dialect (TableGen Spec)
The AionHL dialect bridges AST abstractions and machine code.
def Aion_Dialect : Dialect {
  let name = "aion";
  let summary = "Aion High-Level Dialect for AI-native code generation.";
  let cppNamespace = "::mlir::aion";
}

def Aion_IntentOp : Op<Aion_Dialect, "intent", [IsolatedFromAbove]> {
  let arguments = (ins StringAttr:$goal, StringAttr:$precondition, StringAttr:$postcondition);
}

def Aion_PipeOp : Op<Aion_Dialect, "pipe", [NoSideEffect]> {
  let arguments = (ins AnyType:$input_val, SymbolRefAttr:$target_func);
  let results = (outs AnyType:$output_val);
  let assemblyFormat = "$input_val `|` $target_func attr-dict `:` type($input_val) `->` type($output_val)";
}

def Aion_InferCastOp : Op<Aion_Dialect, "infer_cast", [DeclareOpInterfaceMethods<CastOpInterface>]> {
  let arguments = (ins AnyType:$input);
  let results = (outs AnyType:$resolved_type);
}

def Aion_MatchNullOp : Op<Aion_Dialect, "match_null", [Terminator]> {
  let arguments = (ins AnyType:$target);
  let successors = (successor AnySuccessor:$null_block, AnySuccessor:$val_block);
}

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
8. Environment Setup (Ubuntu / VS Code)
Install Compiler & Backend: Install the LLVM/MLIR-backed Aion compiler via terminal.
LLM API Configuration: Create a .env file with OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to allow the compiler to perform gradual verification.
IDE Integration (DualCode Extension): Install the Aion VS Code extension. Because Aion code lacks visual whitespace formatting to save tokens, the extension provides a bidirectional translator: it renders the code visually readable for humans while passing the raw, dense token stream to the LLM and compiler.
Sandboxing: Install Docker to provide the ephemeral execution environment for verify blocks.
AST Daemon: Run the Aion project initializer to start the background AST repository map service for multi-file context tracking.
